name: SmartAdvocate Personal Injury Case Management API
what_it_does: SmartAdvocate plaintiff personal injury and mass tort case management REST API for high-volume PI firms with advanced case workflow, medical management, and business intelligence. Enables AI agents to manage case intake and client management for high-volume PI firm automation, handle medical treatment tracking and provider contact management for medical documentation automation, access insurance carrier and adjuster management for demand and settlement workflow automation, retrieve statute of limitations and docket deadline tracking for PI litigation automation, manage lien tracking (medical providers, Medicare, Medicaid, health insurers) for settlement disbursement automation, handle case status workflow and milestone automation for firm production management, access fee and cost tracking for contingency fee management automation, retrieve firm productivity and staff performance analytics for law firm KPI reporting, manage document generation and merge templates for case communication automation, and integrate SmartAdvocate with medical records retrieval services, FACS, and court e-filing for PI firm ecosystem automation.
use_cases: Processing PI intakes from high-volume intake agents
Tracking medical treatment from documentation agents
Reporting firm KPIs from analytics agents
not_for: General practice law firm (use Clio or PracticePanther)
best_when: A high-volume plaintiff personal injury, mass tort, or workers compensation law firm wanting AI agents to automate client intake, medical management, insurance carrier workflow, settlement processing, and firm productivity analytics within SmartAdvocate.
avoid_when: MEDICARE SECONDARY PAYER CONDITIONAL PAYMENT RESOLUTION MANDATORY: Automated settlement disbursement for PI cases with Medicare-covered medical treatment must resolve Medicare Secondary Payer conditional payment demand before disbursement; automated disbursement without MSP lien clearance creates personal attorney liability under the Medicare Secondary Payer Act. HIPAA PHI IN MEDICAL DOCUMENT MANAGEMENT: Automated medical record intake, storage, and sharing in SmartAdvocate contains patient PHI obtained under HIPAA authorization for litigation purposes; automated PHI sharing with non-litigation parties or beyond minimum necessary creates HIPAA violation; implement role-based PHI access control in automation. STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS MULTI-JURISDICTION COMPLEXITY: Automated SOL tracking in SmartAdvocate for multi-state PI practice must apply correct state-specific SOL periods and discovery rule exceptions; automated SOL calculation for latent injury (asbestos, toxic tort) cases using standard injury date SOL creates missed deadline and malpractice exposure.

auth.notes: SmartAdvocate uses API key and OAuth authentication. REST API with JSON. New York City, New York HQ. Founded 1999. Private. High-volume plaintiff PI, mass tort, and workers compensation focus. 1,600+ plaintiff law firms. SQL Server database with cloud hosting option. Medical management, lien tracking, and BI analytics differentiation. Competes with Needles Neos, Filevine, and Litify for plaintiff personal injury case management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-user annual subscription; implementation and training required
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual per-user subscription; cloud and on-premise options; implementation and training professional services
pricing.notes: New York NY. Private. Founded 1999. 1,600+ plaintiff PI firms. On-premise and cloud. Per-user annual subscription with implementation.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; case and document queries throttled; webhooks for case status events

known_agent_gotchas: MEDICARE SECONDARY PAYER LIEN RESOLUTION BEFORE DISBURSEMENT: Automated settlement distribution in SmartAdvocate for cases with Medicare-covered treatment must obtain MSP conditional payment final demand and secure release before disbursement; automated bypass of MSP clearance creates attorney personal liability under Medicare Secondary Payer Act enforcement

SQL SERVER ON-PREMISE VS CLOUD API ENDPOINT ARCHITECTURE: SmartAdvocate offers on-premise SQL Server and cloud hosted deployment; API endpoint and network access differ significantly; on-premise SmartAdvocate requires VPN or direct network access from automation host; cloud SmartAdvocate uses standard HTTPS API; verify deployment type before automation architecture

MEDICAL RECORD PHI ACCESS CONTROL FOR LITIGATION PURPOSE: SmartAdvocate medical document management contains patient PHI under HIPAA medical authorization for litigation; automated PHI access must be scoped to litigation team roles; automated medical record export or sharing outside litigation context creates HIPAA minimum necessary violation

SOL tracking for toxic tort and latent injury discovery rule — automated SOL calculation for toxic tort, asbestos, mold, and latent occupational disease cases must apply state-specific discovery rule (SOL runs from date of knowledge of injury and cause, not date of exposure); automated SOL using exposure date instead of discovery date creates systematically missed SOL in latent injury cases

Lien tracking completeness across all payers — automated lien management must capture all lien sources: medical provider liens, Medicare, Medicaid, ERISA health plan subrogation, and workers compensation subrogation; automated settlement disbursement without comprehensive lien inventory creates post-disbursement lien demand and attorney liability

HIPAA BAA for cloud SmartAdvocate PHI processing — SmartAdvocate cloud deployment stores patient PHI for PI case management; automated integrations require HIPAA Business Associate Agreement with SmartAdvocate; automated PHI processing via API without BAA creates HIPAA violation
agent_readiness.security_notes: PI case management. SOC2, HIPAA. API key/OAuth. US. Client PHI, medical records, and settlement data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Plaintiff PI case management. SOC2, HIPAA. API key/OAuth. US. Client PHI, medical, and settlement data.
